  • Tutorial 3: CPW Parameter Sweeps with SweepConfig

Tutorial 3: CPW Parameter Sweeps with SweepConfig¶

Module: simpleLOMs.sweeps

Use this API when you want tidy pandas tables of \(f_0\), \(\kappa\), \(Q\), or load shifts while varying geometry or circuit parameters one at a time.

Key ideas:

  • SweepConfig holds a complete immutable device snapshot

  • sweep() varies one field via dataclasses.replace

  • LC models refit when d or cpw_params change; otherwise L/C can be reused for speed

[ ]:

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import skrf as rf

from simpleLOMs import CPWParams, SweepConfig, sweep, sweep_length, sweep_coupling, sweep_load_frequency

print("Imports OK")

1. Define a base configuration¶

[ ]:

base = SweepConfig(
    cpw_params=CPWParams(
        w=11.7e-6, s=5.1e-6, t=0.0, h=500e-6,
        rho=1e-19, ep_r=11.45, has_metal_backside=True, tand=0.0,
    ),
    freq=rf.Frequency(4e9, 12e9, 10_001, unit="Hz"),
    d=7e-3,
    Cc1=5e-15, Cc2=5e-15,
    Ctog1=1e-14, Ctog2=1e-14,
    Lload1=5e-10, Cload1=6e-13,
    Lload2=5e-10, Cload2=6e-13,
    Z0=50.0,
)

base

2. Sweep resonator length (CPW ground truth)¶

sweep_length always refits because d changes the underlying resonance.

[ ]:

d_values = np.linspace(5.5e-3, 8.5e-3, 12)
df_length = sweep_length(
    d_values,
    base,
    extract=["f0_s11", "kappa_s11", "Q_s11"],
    model="cpw",
)
df_length.head()

[ ]:

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
ax.plot(df_length["d"] * 1e3, df_length["f0_s11"], "o-")
ax.set_xlabel("Resonator length (mm)")
ax.set_ylabel("f0 S11 (GHz)")
ax.set_title("CPW resonance vs length")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

3. Sweep coupling capacitance with FosterFit¶

When sweeping Cc1, FosterFit can reuse L/C fitted at the first point (fast).

[ ]:

cc_values = np.linspace(2e-15, 15e-15, 10)
df_coupling = sweep_coupling(
    cc_values,
    base,
    extract=["f0_s11", "kappa_s11"],
    model="foster",
    symmetric=False,  # sweep Cc1 only; Cc2 stays fixed
)
df_coupling.head()

4. Sweep load frequency¶

sweep_load_frequency adjusts Lload1 (holding Cload1 fixed) to target a bare load resonance.

[ ]:

f_targets_hz = np.linspace(2.5e9, 4.5e9, 9)
df_load = sweep_load_frequency(
    f_targets_hz,
    base,
    extract=["f0_s11", "shift_mode1", "shift_mode2", "shift_mode3"],
    model="foster",
    side="1",
)
df_load.head()

[ ]:

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
ax.plot(df_load["f_load_GHz"], df_load["shift_mode2"], "o-", label="mode 2 shift")
ax.set_xlabel("Target load 1 frequency (GHz)")
ax.set_ylabel("Hybridization shift (GHz)")
ax.legend()
ax.set_title("FosterFit loaded-network shifts")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

5. Generic sweep() API¶

Any SweepConfig field can be swept explicitly:

[ ]:

df_generic = sweep(
    "Ctog1",
    np.linspace(5e-15, 30e-15, 8),
    base,
    extract=["f0_s11", "kappa_s11"],
    model="optimized",
    refit=False,
)
df_generic.tail(3)

When to use sweeps vs system / load_sweeps¶

Goal

Module

Compare Foster/Optimized/Analytical against CPW with shifts

analyze_system

Cc grid at fixed loads

run_accuracy_sweep

Many load-frequency pairs per device

run_accuracy_sweep_load_grid

Design exploration tables (\(f_0\), \(\kappa\), \(Q\), shifts vs one knob)

sweeps
